text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Regular consumption of nuts has been associated with a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease in several large prospective cohort studies.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Regular consumption of nuts has been associated with a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease in several large prospective cohort studies.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states the association between regular nut consumption and lower cardiovascular disease risk.","population":"","intervention":"regular consumption of nuts","comparator":"","outcome":"reduced risk of cardiovascular disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"In the Nurses' Health Study, women who ate nuts five or more times per week had a 35% lower risk of coronary heart disease than women who rarely ate nuts.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"In the Nurses' Health Study, women who ate nuts five or more times per week had a 35% lower risk of coronary heart disease than women who rarely ate nuts.","entailment":"The sentence provides the observed risk reduction for women with frequent nut intake compared to rare consumers.","population":"women in the Nurses' Health Study","intervention":"ate nuts five or more times per week","comparator":"women who rarely ate nuts","outcome":"35% lower risk of coronary heart disease","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"35% lower risk","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"A 2013 randomized controlled trial (PREDIMED) found that a Mediterranean diet supplemented with mixed nuts reduced the incidence of major cardiovascular events by approximately 28% compared with a control diet.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"A 2013 randomized controlled trial (PREDIMED) found that a Mediterranean diet supplemented with mixed nuts reduced the incidence of major cardiovascular events by approximately 28% compared with a control diet.","entailment":"The RCT result directly reports a causal reduction in major cardiovascular events due to nut‑supplemented diet versus control.","population":"participants in the PREDIMED trial","intervention":"Mediterranean diet supplemented with mixed nuts","comparator":"control diet","outcome":"reduced incidence of major cardiovascular events by approximately 28%","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"approximately 28%","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Observational findings may be confounded because nut consumers in these cohorts tended to exercise more, smoke less, and have higher incomes.","claimType":"methodological","quote":"Observational findings may be confounded: nut consumers in these cohorts tended to exercise more, smoke less, and have higher incomes.","entailment":"The sentence points out potential confounding variables associated with nut consumption that could bias observational results.","population":"nut consumers in the cohorts","intervention":"nut consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"tended to exercise more, smoke less, and have higher incomes","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Critics note that the PREDIMED trial was partially re‑analysed in 2018 after irregularities in randomization at some sites, although the corrected analysis reported similar effect sizes.","claimType":"methodological","quote":"Critics also note that the PREDIMED trial was partially re-analysed in 2018 after irregularities in randomization at some sites, although the corrected analysis reported similar effect sizes.","entailment":"The passage describes a methodological issue (re‑analysis due to randomization problems) and that the revised results remained comparable.","population":"PREDIMED trial","intervention":"partial re‑analysis after randomization irregularities","comparator":"","outcome":"corrected analysis reported similar effect sizes","timeframe":"2018","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Nuts are
